fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer dietitian license to georgia, dietitian compact georgia, georgia dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by March 31, even years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in Georgia, regulated by the Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians under the Secretary of State.

license_types.title: Provisional Permit
license_types.description: Allows practice under supervision of a Georgia-licensed dietitian for applicants who are eligible to take the CDR exam but have not yet passed it. Valid for one year and may be renewed at Board discretion. Additional $25 fee.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a baccalaureate or higher degree with a major in dietetics, human nutrition, or a related field from a regionally accredited institution. Complete at least 900 hours of supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited program.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national Registration Examination for Dietitians.

steps.title: Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Create an account on the Georgia Secretary of State's online licensing portal and submit the completed application with the non-refundable $85 fee (includes $10 processing fee).

steps.title: Provide Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload official transcripts, a copy of your current CDR registration card or award letter, and your ACEND Verification Statement. If applying by endorsement, include license verification from all other states.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.

documents.item: CDR Registration Card or Award Letter
documents.detail: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
Verifying completion of supervised practice experience.

documents.item: Secure & Verifiable Document and Affidavit of Citizenship
documents.detail: As required by Georgia law for professional licensing.

provisional_text: Georgia offers a provisional permit for applicants who have completed all academic and supervised practice requirements and are eligible for but have not yet passed the CDR examination. The permit costs an additional $25, is valid for one year, and requires practice under the direct supervision of a Georgia-licensed dietitian. The Board may renew it at its discretion.
reciprocity_text: Georgia offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians holding a valid, active, and unencumbered license in another state, provided that state's requirements are substantially equal to or greater than Georgia's. Applicants must submit their application, fee, verification of the out-of-state license sent directly from the issuing board, and proof of passing the CDR exam.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $85 (including a $10 processing fee). If you also request a provisional permit, add $25 for a total of $110. The biennial renewal fee is $60, due by March 31st of even-numbered years.

faqs.question: Which Georgia agency regulates dietitians?
faqs.answer: The Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians, which operates under the Georgia Secretary of State, is the regulatory body for dietitian licensure in the state.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Georgia dietitians?
faqs.answer: You must complete 30 clock hours of continuing education during each two-year renewal cycle. As of 2026, all CE must be tracked and reported through CE Broker.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Georgia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 15 to 20 Business Days. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Georgia offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians holding a valid, active, and unencumbered license in another state, provided that state's requirements are substantially equal to or greater than Georgia's. Applicants must submit their application, fee, verification of the out-of-state license sent directly from the issuing board, and proof of passing the CDR exam.
